title: lichen_util.h lichen_link_strerror depends on LICHEN_EAUTH conditionally
description:
## Location
firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_util.h:145-147

## Finding
The `lichen_link_strerror()` function has a case for `LICHEN_EAUTH` conditionally compiled:
```c
#if HAVE_LICHEN_ERRNO
    case -LICHEN_EAUTH:
        return "authentication failed";
#endif
```

If `CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK` is disabled, `LICHEN_EAUTH` errors will fall through to "unknown error". This is correct behavior (LICHEN_EAUTH can only be returned by link layer code which is also disabled), but the silent fallback could mask issues during development.

## What goes wrong
During development/debugging, if someone calls a function that returns LICHEN_EAUTH in a configuration where CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK is disabled, the error message will be "unknown error" instead of the specific authentication failure message.

## Recommendation
Low priority. The current design is intentional (lichen_l2.c requires CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK via BUILD_ASSERT). No action needed unless LICHEN_EAUTH becomes usable outside the link layer.
close_reason: Documented build configuration. The conditional is correct: LICHEN_EAUTH only exists when CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK is enabled. Added comments explaining the build config dependency at both the #define HAVE_LICHEN_ERRNO location and the switch case.
